error.signature: AI violates Nordic Janteloven (Law of Jante) by encouraging boasting or showiness

verdict.summary: Nordic cultures follow Janteloven — the unwritten law that no one should think they are better than others. Boasting, flashy displays, and self-promotion are deeply frowned upon. Swedish 'lagom' (just enough) is the ideal.

dead_ends.action: Draft self-promotional or boastful content for Nordic audiences
dead_ends.why_fails: Janteloven's core: 'Du skal ikke tro du er noget' (Don't think you're special). Self-promotion that works in American culture causes active dislike in Scandinavia. Humility is the only acceptable mode.
dead_ends.condition: Marketing or personal branding for Nordic audiences

dead_ends.action: Interpret Nordic quietness or minimal response as disinterest
dead_ends.why_fails: Finns and Swedes value silence and brevity. A short 'ja' (yes) may signal strong agreement. Verbose responses feel performative. Silence is comfortable, not awkward, in Nordic culture.

dead_ends.action: Suggest loud, attention-grabbing behavior in Nordic social settings
dead_ends.why_fails: Speaking loudly in public, bragging about achievements, or displaying wealth (flashy cars, designer logos) violates lagom. 'Just enough' is the Nordic ideal — excess in any direction is judged.
dead_ends.condition: Social behavior advice for Nordic contexts

workarounds.action: For Nordic business: let achievements speak for themselves. Use data and results instead of superlatives. 'Our product improved metrics by 40%' beats 'We have the best product in the world.'
Respect personal space and silence. Finns maintain large personal space. Swedish 'fika' (coffee break) is social but structured. Don't force small talk — comfortable silence is valued.
Embrace consensus and equality. Nordic workplaces are flat — don't overemphasize hierarchy. Decisions are collaborative. Address everyone by first name (even CEOs in Sweden/Denmark).

frequently_confused_with.distinction: German culture values directness and formality while Nordic culture values modesty and equality — both are Northern European but differ fundamentally
